summary: Seven new comment-endorsed tools and learning resources from 30 current Hacker News front-page stories.
telegram_footer: 7 items surfaced | 30/30 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: OPNsense
items.source_story_title: HomeLab #1: MikroTik as a Home Router
items.summary: An open-source firewall and routing platform recommended as a capable homelab alternative.
items.why_included: A commenter gave a concrete home-router build recommendation and said it had been running like a dream.
items.evidence: In the MikroTik discussion, a commenter said homelab users are far better served installing OPNsense on repurposed Sophos hardware.

items.name: WinBox
items.source_story_title: HomeLab #1: MikroTik as a Home Router
items.summary: A cross-platform desktop utility for configuring MikroTik RouterOS devices.
items.why_included: Multiple users independently praised the newer cross-platform WinBox v4 experience.
items.evidence: One commenter called the all-platform WinBox release a huge QoL improvement; another said v4 is much nicer and runs on Linux.

items.name: Orion Browser
items.summary: A Kagi browser beta with built-in ad blocking and nested vertical tabs.
items.why_included: A Linux user gave specific positive first-hand feedback on features that replace Firefox extensions.
items.evidence: A commenter wrote that the Linux beta feels nice and that its ad blocking and nested tabs meet their main Firefox needs.

items.name: The Riemann Hypothesis, Part 1
items.source_story_title: What does the Riemann zeta function have to do with the distribution of primes?
items.summary: An introductory article series explaining the Riemann hypothesis and prime distribution.
items.why_included: It was directly recommended as a more approachable explanation than the submitted article.
items.evidence: A commenter said they had personally found this overview much more approachable for the question in the title.

items.name: OTR Food & History
items.source_story_title: Bananas sprout in Rayleigh Garden UK after 15 years
items.summary: A long-form YouTube documentary channel about food and global history.
items.why_included: The channel received a direct, enthusiastic first-hand recommendation with a specific banana-history documentary.
items.evidence: A commenter called OTR their favorite YouTube long-form documentary maker and recommended its History of the Banana video.

items.name: Technology Connections
items.source_story_title: Bananas sprout in Rayleigh Garden UK after 15 years
items.summary: A YouTube channel explaining everyday technology through detailed video essays.
items.why_included: It received an unambiguous recommendation alongside other high-quality long-form channels.
items.evidence: A commenter called Technology Connections one of the rare gems among YouTube channels.

items.name: Alexander the OK
items.source_story_title: Bananas sprout in Rayleigh Garden UK after 15 years
items.summary: A YouTube channel recommended for thoughtful long-form video essays.
items.why_included: It was explicitly named as one of a commenter's rare YouTube-channel gems.
items.evidence: A commenter grouped Alexander the OK with channels they described as rare gems among YouTube channels.
